name_policy: Personal names remain in original English.
spoiler_policy: Only information available through each current paragraph is used.

blocks.original_text: The summons came in the hour before the dawn, when the world was still and grey.
blocks.explanation_zh: 天亮前世界仍安静灰暗时，国王的召唤突然到来。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 世界“still and grey”时召唤抵达，国事先以打断睡眠的方式出现；灰色黎明也为全章道德争论定调。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: Alyn shook him roughly from his dreams and Ned stumbled into the predawn chill, groggy from sleep, to find his horse saddled and the king already mounted. Robert wore thick brown gloves and a heavy fur cloak with a hood that covered his ears, and looked for all the world like a bear sitting a horse. “Up, Stark!” he roared. “Up, up! We have matters of state to discuss.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Alyn把睡梦中的Eddard叫醒；他走进寒冷，发现Robert已经骑在马上，穿着厚重毛皮，像一头骑马的熊，并高声说有国事要谈。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert被比作骑马的熊，强调他体格、毛皮与吼声构成的粗野活力，与刚醒的Eddard形成反差。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “By all means,” Ned said. “Come inside, Your Grace.” Alyn lifted the flap of the tent.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard请Robert进入帐篷交谈，Alyn也替他们掀开门帘。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段从秘密晨骑转入少年时代怀旧、婚姻责任与Jon母亲的话题，再以First Men古墓将私人往事连接到北方长久历史。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “No, no, no,” Robert said. His breath steamed with every word. “The camp is full of ears. Besides, I want to ride out and taste this country of yours.” Ser Boros and Ser Meryn waited behind him with a dozen guardsmen, Ned saw. There was nothing to do but rub the sleep from his eyes, dress, and mount up.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert拒绝进入，因为营地到处有耳朵，也想骑马体验北方；面对等候的Kingsguard与卫兵，Eddard只能穿衣上马。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert说营地“到处有耳朵”，既说明密报敏感，也说明国王即使在自己队伍中仍需主动制造私密空间。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: Robert set the pace, driving his huge black destrier hard as Ned galloped along beside him, trying to keep up. He called out a question as they rode, but the wind blew his words away, and the king did not hear him. After that Ned rode in silence. They soon left the kingsroad and took off across rolling plains dark with mist. By then the guard had fallen back a small distance, safely out of earshot, but still Robert would not slow.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert催动黑色战马高速前进，Eddard努力跟随；风吹散说话声，他们离开kingsroad进入雾中平原，护卫退到听不见的位置。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert用速度而非命令拉开与护卫的距离；风吹走Eddard的话，使谈话开始前先出现无法被听见的形象。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: Dawn broke as they crested a low ridge, and finally the king pulled up. By then they were miles south of the main party. Robert was flushed and exhilarated as Ned reined up beside him. “Gods,” he swore, laughing, “it feels good to get out and ride the way a man was meant to ride! I swear, Ned, this creeping along is enough to drive a man mad.” He had never been a patient man, Robert Baratheon. “That damnable wheelhouse, the way it creaks and groans, climbing every bump in the road as if it were a mountain . . . I promise you, if that wretched thing breaks another axle, I’m going to burn it, and Cersei can walk!”
blocks.explanation_zh: 两人在山脊上迎来黎明，Robert终于停下，因自由奔驰而兴奋，又抱怨缓慢的wheelhouse，威胁它再断车轴便烧掉，让Cersei步行。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert把快骑想象成“男人本应有的方式”，把缓慢车队和家庭责任体验为束缚。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: Ned laughed. “I will gladly light the torch for you.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard笑着说自己很乐意替他点火。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段从秘密晨骑转入少年时代怀旧、婚姻责任与Jon母亲的话题，再以First Men古墓将私人往事连接到北方长久历史。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “Good man!” The king clapped him on the shoulder. “I’ve half a mind to leave them all behind and just keep going.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert拍他的肩，说自己甚至想把所有人抛下，一直骑下去。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段从秘密晨骑转入少年时代怀旧、婚姻责任与Jon母亲的话题，再以First Men古墓将私人往事连接到北方长久历史。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: A smile touched Ned’s lips. “I do believe you mean it.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard微笑说，他相信Robert是认真的。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段从秘密晨骑转入少年时代怀旧、婚姻责任与Jon母亲的话题，再以First Men古墓将私人往事连接到北方长久历史。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “I do, I do,” the king said. “What do you say, Ned? Just you and me, two vagabond knights on the kingsroad, our swords at our sides and the gods know what in front of us, and maybe a farmer’s daughter or a tavern wench to warm our beds tonight.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert幻想二人像从前一样做流浪骑士，带剑沿路冒险，再找农家女或酒馆女郎陪伴。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 流浪骑士幻想并非真正计划，而是Robert对失去的少年时代、性自由和无责任生活的怀旧。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “Would that we could,” Ned said, “but we have duties now, my liege . . . to the realm, to our children, I to my lady wife and you to your queen. We are not the boys we were.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard承认那种生活令人向往，却提醒他们如今对王国、孩子和妻子都有责任，早已不是少年。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard用职责逐项回应幻想：realm、children、wife、queen；两人的根本差异是如何看待成年身份。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “You were never the boy you were,” Robert grumbled. “More’s the pity. And yet there was that one time . . . what was her name, that common girl of yours? Becca? No, she was one of mine, gods love her, black hair and these sweet big eyes, you could drown in them. Yours was . . . Aleena? No. You told me once. Was it Merryl? You know the one I mean, your bastard’s mother?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert抱怨Eddard从未真正年轻过，随后回忆自己和Eddard过去认识的女子，最终追问Jon的母亲。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert对女人名字的混乱既带喜剧效果，也显示他的众多短暂关系；追问Jon母亲却触碰Eddard不愿娱乐化的记忆。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “Her name was Wylla,” Ned replied with cool courtesy, “and I would sooner not speak of her.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard冷淡而有礼地回答她叫Wylla，并表示不想谈论。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段从秘密晨骑转入少年时代怀旧、婚姻责任与Jon母亲的话题，再以First Men古墓将私人往事连接到北方长久历史。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “Wylla. Yes.” The king grinned. “She must have been a rare wench if she could make Lord Eddard Stark forget his honor, even for an hour. You never told me what she looked like . . . ”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert笑称能令Eddard暂时忘记荣誉的女人必定非同寻常，又想追问她的外貌。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段从秘密晨骑转入少年时代怀旧、婚姻责任与Jon母亲的话题，再以First Men古墓将私人往事连接到北方长久历史。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: Ned’s mouth tightened in anger. “Nor will I. Leave it be, Robert, for the love you say you bear me. I dishonored myself and I dishonored Catelyn, in the sight of gods and men.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard因愤怒收紧嘴角，请Robert看在友谊份上住口，因为他认为自己在神与人面前同时羞辱了自己和Catelyn。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard不把自己的行为解释为男性常态，而将其命名为对自己和Catelyn的双重羞辱，荣誉在这里首先是责任。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “Gods have mercy, you scarcely knew Catelyn.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert辩称Eddard当时几乎还不了解Catelyn。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段从秘密晨骑转入少年时代怀旧、婚姻责任与Jon母亲的话题，再以First Men古墓将私人往事连接到北方长久历史。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “I had taken her to wife. She was carrying my child.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard回答，他已经娶她为妻，而且她正怀着他的孩子。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段从秘密晨骑转入少年时代怀旧、婚姻责任与Jon母亲的话题，再以First Men古墓将私人往事连接到北方长久历史。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “You are too hard on yourself, Ned. You always were. Damn it, no woman wants Baelor the Blessed in her bed.” He slapped a hand on his knee. “Well, I’ll not press you if you feel so strong about it, though I swear, at times you’re so prickly you ought to take the hedgehog as your sigil.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert说Eddard一向对自己过于严厉，又用Baelor the Blessed和刺猬家徽开玩笑，最后答应不再追问。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 刺猬家徽玩笑用heraldry语言形容Eddard“扎手”，Robert通过调笑结束一场自己无法理解的严肃自责。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: The rising sun sent fingers of light through the pale white mists of dawn. A wide plain spread out beneath them, bare and brown, its flatness here and there relieved by long, low hummocks. Ned pointed them out to his king. “The barrows of the First Men.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 初升太阳穿过白雾照亮褐色平原，Eddard指出地面低矮长丘是First Men的barrows。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 阳光像手指穿过雾，古墓丘在视野中显现；私人旧事自然过渡到埋葬历史的土地。
blocks.background_zh: **历史背景：** barrows是覆盖古代墓葬的土丘；北方遍布First Men古墓，显示土地的人类历史极其久远。

blocks.original_text: Robert frowned. “Have we ridden onto a graveyard?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert皱眉问他们是不是骑进了墓地。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段从秘密晨骑转入少年时代怀旧、婚姻责任与Jon母亲的话题，再以First Men古墓将私人往事连接到北方长久历史。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “There are barrows everywhere in the north, Your Grace,” Ned told him. “This land is old.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard说北方到处都有barrows，因为这片土地非常古老。
blocks.literary_note_zh: “This land is old”极其简短，却把北方身份从寒冷地理扩展为对远古死者与长时段记忆的意识。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “And cold,” Robert grumbled, pulling his cloak more tightly around himself. The guard had reined up well behind them, at the bottom of the ridge. “Well, I did not bring you out here to talk of graves or bicker about your bastard. There was a rider in the night, from Lord Varys in King’s Landing. Here.” The king pulled a paper from his belt and handed it to Ned.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert抱怨这里也很冷，随后说明夜里有King’s Landing信使带来Varys的密报，并把纸交给Eddard。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段由Varys密报引出Jorah和Daenerys婚讯；Robert提出暗杀，Eddard则以儿童无辜和跨海物流反驳复仇冲动。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: Varys the eunuch was the king’s master of whisperers. He served Robert now as he had once served Aerys Targaryen. Ned unrolled the paper with trepidation, thinking of Lysa and her terrible accusation, but the message did not concern Lady Arryn. “What is the source for this information?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard解释Varys是master of whisperers，曾先后服务Aerys和Robert；他担心信与Lysa指控有关，但读后发现是另一件事，并追问消息来源。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard看到Varys来信时先想到Lysa，说明她的指控已成为他解读所有王都消息的首要框架。
blocks.background_zh: **职务：** master of whisperers负责情报与间谍网络；Varys曾服务Aerys，如今继续服务Robert。

blocks.original_text: “Do you remember Ser Jorah Mormont?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert问Eddard是否记得Ser Jorah Mormont。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段由Varys密报引出Jorah和Daenerys婚讯；Robert提出暗杀，Eddard则以儿童无辜和跨海物流反驳复仇冲动。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “Would that I might forget him,” Ned said bluntly. The Mormonts of Bear Island were an old house, proud and honorable, but their lands were cold and distant and poor. Ser Jorah had tried to swell the family coffers by selling some poachers to a Tyroshi slaver. As the Mormonts were bannermen to the Starks, his crime had dishonored the north. Ned had made the long journey west to Bear Island, only to find when he arrived that Jorah had taken ship beyond the reach of Ice and the king’s justice. Five years had passed since then.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard希望自己能忘记：Jorah曾把偷猎者卖给Tyroshi奴隶贩子，给北境和House Mormont蒙羞；Eddard赶去执行国王法律时，他已乘船逃亡。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Jorah罪行不是抽象违法：作为Stark bannerman，他使北境领主必须亲自跨越长途执行司法，也把家族贫困与奴隶交易联系起来。
blocks.background_zh: **文本事实：** Jorah把偷猎者卖给Tyroshi奴隶贩子，违反王国法律；作为Stark bannerman，本应由Eddard执行判决，但他已逃亡。

blocks.original_text: “Ser Jorah is now in Pentos, anxious to earn a royal pardon that would allow him to return from exile,” Robert explained. “Lord Varys makes good use of him.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert解释Jorah如今身在Pentos，希望以情报换取王室赦免，因此被Varys利用。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段由Varys密报引出Jorah和Daenerys婚讯；Robert提出暗杀，Eddard则以儿童无辜和跨海物流反驳复仇冲动。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “So the slaver has become a spy,” Ned said with distaste. He handed the letter back. “I would rather he become a corpse.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard厌恶地说奴隶贩子如今变成间谍，他宁愿Jorah成为尸体。
blocks.literary_note_zh: “slaver变成spy”的平行句式并不表示赎罪，只表示一种不光彩身份被另一种替代。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “Varys tells me that spies are more useful than corpses,” Robert said. “Jorah aside, what do you make of his report?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert说Varys认为间谍比尸体有用，随后要求Eddard评价报告内容。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段由Varys密报引出Jorah和Daenerys婚讯；Robert提出暗杀，Eddard则以儿童无辜和跨海物流反驳复仇冲动。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “Daenerys Targaryen has wed some Dothraki horselord. What of it? Shall we send her a wedding gift?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard说报告只是Daenerys嫁给一名Dothraki horselord，并讽刺是否该送结婚礼物。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段由Varys密报引出Jorah和Daenerys婚讯；Robert提出暗杀，Eddard则以儿童无辜和跨海物流反驳复仇冲动。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: The king frowned. “A knife, perhaps. A good sharp one, and a bold man to wield it.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert阴沉地建议送一把利刀，以及一个敢于用刀的人。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert把Eddard的“婚礼礼物”讽刺接成暗杀建议，几乎不经过过渡，暴露他已预设报告只能导向杀戮。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: Ned did not feign surprise; Robert’s hatred of the Targaryens was a madness in him. He remembered the angry words they had exchanged when Tywin Lannister had presented Robert with the corpses of Rhaegar’s wife and children as a token of fealty. Ned had named that murder; Robert called it war. When he had protested that the young prince and princess were no more than babes, his new-made king had replied, “I see no babes. Only dragonspawn.” Not even Jon Arryn had been able to calm that storm. Eddard Stark had ridden out that very day in a cold rage, to fight the last battles of the war alone in the south. It had taken another death to reconcile them; Lyanna’s death, and the grief they had shared over her passing.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard并不惊讶，因为Robert对Targaryens的仇恨近乎疯狂；他回忆Tywin把Rhaegar妻儿尸体献来时，自己称之为谋杀，Robert却称为战争，两人直到Lyanna死后才因共同悲痛和解。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 回忆把当下争论放进旧裂痕：Eddard曾因儿童尸体离开Robert，二人的和解来自共同失去Lyanna，而非对谋杀达成一致。
blocks.background_zh: **历史背景：** Tywin在叛乱末期把Rhaegar妻儿尸体献给Robert。Eddard称之为谋杀，Robert称之为战争，两人曾因此决裂。

blocks.original_text: This time, Ned resolved to keep his temper. “Your Grace, the girl is scarcely more than a child. You are no Tywin Lannister, to slaughter innocents.” It was said that Rhaegar’s little girl had cried as they dragged her from beneath her bed to face the swords. The boy had been no more than a babe in arms, yet Lord Tywin’s soldiers had torn him from his mother’s breast and dashed his head against a wall.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard决定克制脾气，提醒Robert那个女孩仍是孩子，他不该像Tywin一样屠杀无辜；同时想起Rhaegar幼子女当年被杀的惨状。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard刻意称Daenerys为“girl”和“child”，用年龄抵抗Robert把她抽象成Targaryen威胁的语言。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “And how long will this one remain an innocent?” Robert’s mouth grew hard. “This child will soon enough spread her legs and start breeding more dragonspawn to plague me.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert反问她还能保持无辜多久，并以粗暴语言将她未来可能生育的孩子称为dragonspawn。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert用粗暴性语言把未来可能性说成现成罪行，并将尚不存在的孩子也预先归类为dragonspawn。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “Nonetheless,” Ned said, “the murder of children . . . it would be vile . . . unspeakable . . . ”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard坚持杀害儿童是卑劣且不可言说的行为。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段由Varys密报引出Jorah和Daenerys婚讯；Robert提出暗杀，Eddard则以儿童无辜和跨海物流反驳复仇冲动。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “Unspeakable?” the king roared. “What Aerys did to your brother Brandon was unspeakable. The way your lord father died, that was unspeakable. And Rhaegar . . . how many times do you think he raped your sister? How many hundreds of times?” His voice had grown so loud that his horse whinnied nervously beneath him. The king jerked the reins hard, quieting the animal, and pointed an angry finger at Ned. “I will kill every Targaryen I can get my hands on, until they are as dead as their dragons, and then I will piss on their graves.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert咆哮说Aerys杀害Brandon和Eddard父亲、Rhaegar伤害Lyanna才真正不可言说，并发誓杀尽所有Targaryens。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert把Eddard的“不可言说”夺来描述自己家族遭遇；这不是反驳杀童本身，而是以更早伤害为复仇授权。
blocks.background_zh: **人物所述：** Robert将Brandon、Eddard父亲和Lyanna的遭遇归罪Targaryens；关于Lyanna经历的具体全貌截至本段仍主要来自Robert的愤怒叙述。

blocks.original_text: Ned knew better than to defy him when the wrath was on him. If the years had not quenched Robert’s thirst for revenge, no words of his would help. “You can’t get your hands on this one, can you?” he said quietly.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard知道Robert盛怒时无法被说服，便避开道德争论，平静指出Daenerys如今并不在他能触及的地方。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard知道道德劝告在盛怒时无效，便改用现实可达性问题；这是政治沟通策略，不是价值退让。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: The king’s mouth twisted in a bitter grimace. “No, gods be cursed. Some pox-ridden Pentoshi cheesemonger had her brother and her walled up on his estate with pointy-hatted eunuchs all around them, and now he’s handed them over to the Dothraki. I should have had them both killed years ago, when it was easy to get at them, but Jon was as bad as you. More fool I, I listened to him.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert苦涩承认无法下手，抱怨Illyrio曾保护两名Targaryen、如今又把他们交给Dothraki，并后悔当年听Jon Arryn劝告没有杀掉他们。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert用疾病、食物和异国服饰侮辱Illyrio及其护卫，语言贬低成为无法实际攻击目标时的替代行动。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “Jon Arryn was a wise man and a good Hand.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard说Jon Arryn是一位明智的人和优秀的Hand。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段由Varys密报引出Jorah和Daenerys婚讯；Robert提出暗杀，Eddard则以儿童无辜和跨海物流反驳复仇冲动。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: Robert snorted. The anger was leaving him as suddenly as it had come. “This Khal Drogo is said to have a hundred thousand men in his horde. What would Jon say to that?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert怒气迅速消退，转而说Drogo据称有十万武士，并问Jon Arryn会如何判断威胁。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert怒气“突然离开”与突然到来一样快，显示他的情绪高度剧烈却不持久；国家决策因此可能被瞬时激情推动。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “He would say that even a million Dothraki are no threat to the realm, so long as they remain on the other side of the narrow sea,” Ned replied calmly. “The barbarians have no ships. They hate and fear the open sea.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard冷静回答，只要Dothraki留在narrow sea另一边，哪怕一百万也不是威胁，因为他们没有船且惧怕开放海域。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard的判断以物流而非勇气为基础：没有船、畏惧海洋，使人数无法自动转化为跨海军事威胁。
blocks.background_zh: **地理事实：** narrow sea隔开Seven Kingdoms与Free Cities；Dothraki没有自己的舰队，且文化上畏惧开放海域。

blocks.original_text: The king shifted uncomfortably in his saddle. “Perhaps. There are ships to be had in the Free Cities, though. I tell you, Ned, I do not like this marriage. There are still those in the Seven Kingdoms who call me Usurper. Do you forget how many houses fought for Targaryen in the war? They bide their time for now, but give them half a chance, they will murder me in my bed, and my sons with me. If the beggar king crosses with a Dothraki horde at his back, the traitors will join him.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert仍不安，指出Free Cities有船，也担心国内仍称他Usurper的Targaryen旧部会在Viserys带Dothraki渡海时响应。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert的恐惧并非全无根据：他担心的不只是外敌人数，而是外部军队与国内旧忠诚结合。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “He will not cross,” Ned promised. “And if by some mischance he does, we will throw him back into the sea. Once you choose a new Warden of the East—”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard保证Viserys不会渡海，即使成功也会被赶回海中，随后把话题引向新的Warden of the East。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段从外部Dothraki威胁转入Warden of the East任命，Eddard逐步逼出Jaime已获承诺的事实，并警告Lannister权力集中。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: The king groaned. “For the last time, I will not name the Arryn boy Warden. I know the boy is your nephew, but with Targaryens climbing in bed with Dothraki, I would be mad to rest one quarter of the realm on the shoulders of a sickly child.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert再次拒绝任命年幼病弱的Robert Arryn，认为Targaryen与Dothraki结盟时不能把四分之一王国交给孩子。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert Arryn的法定继承关系与实际军事能力发生冲突；Warden头衔在这里不是纯荣誉，而是国防权力。
blocks.background_zh: **制度：** Warden of the East承担东部国防与军事统辖，不只是继承荣誉；Robert Arryn虽继承House Arryn，却年幼体弱。

blocks.original_text: Ned was ready for that. “Yet we still must have a Warden of the East. If Robert Arryn will not do, name one of your brothers. Stannis proved himself at the siege of Storm’s End, surely.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard已有准备，建议若不用Robert Arryn，便任命Robert的兄弟，例如在Storm’s End围城中证明过自己的Stannis。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段从外部Dothraki威胁转入Warden of the East任命，Eddard逐步逼出Jaime已获承诺的事实，并警告Lannister权力集中。
blocks.background_zh: **文本事实：** Stannis Baratheon是Robert的兄弟，曾在Storm’s End围城中证明军事能力。

blocks.original_text: He let the name hang there for a moment. The king frowned and said nothing. He looked uncomfortable.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard让Stannis的名字悬在空气中，观察Robert皱眉沉默和明显不适。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard故意让Stannis的名字悬停，阅读Robert的沉默和不适；谈判中的停顿本身成为证据。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “That is,” Ned finished quietly, watching, “unless you have already promised the honor to another.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard平静地补充，除非Robert已经把这项荣誉许给别人。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段从外部Dothraki威胁转入Warden of the East任命，Eddard逐步逼出Jaime已获承诺的事实，并警告Lannister权力集中。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: For a moment Robert had the grace to look startled. Just as quickly, the look became annoyance. “What if I have?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert短暂惊讶，随即恼怒，反问即使自己已承诺又如何。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段从外部Dothraki威胁转入Warden of the East任命，Eddard逐步逼出Jaime已获承诺的事实，并警告Lannister权力集中。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “It’s Jaime Lannister, is it not?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard直接猜出被任命者是Jaime Lannister。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段从外部Dothraki威胁转入Warden of the East任命，Eddard逐步逼出Jaime已获承诺的事实，并警告Lannister权力集中。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: Robert kicked his horse back into motion and started down the ridge toward the barrows. Ned kept pace with him. The king rode on, eyes straight ahead. “Yes,” he said at last. A single hard word to end the matter.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert重新催马下山，不看Eddard，最后用一个僵硬的“Yes”确认。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert不看Eddard，以一个硬邦邦的“Yes”结束问题；身体回避和单词长度都显示他知道这项决定难以辩护。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “Kingslayer,” Ned said. The rumors were true, then. He rode on dangerous ground now, he knew. “An able and courageous man, no doubt,” he said carefully, “but his father is Warden of the West, Robert. In time Ser Jaime will succeed to that honor. No one man should hold both East and West.” He left unsaid his real concern; that the appointment would put half the armies of the realm into the hands of Lannisters.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard称Jaime为Kingslayer，并谨慎指出Tywin已是Warden of the West，Jaime将来可能继任，任何人都不该同时掌握东西两方；他没有说出的真正担忧是Lannisters会控制半数军队。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard公开论证权力集中，内心担忧则更明确：任命会让Lannisters实际控制半数军队。
blocks.background_zh: **制度：** Tywin是Warden of the West；若Jaime未来继承西部又现任东部，Lannisters可能控制王国东西两大军事区。

blocks.original_text: “I will fight that battle when the enemy appears on the field,” the king said stubbornly. “At the moment, Lord Tywin looms eternal as Casterly Rock, so I doubt that Jaime will be succeeding anytime soon. Don’t vex me about this, Ned, the stone has been set.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert固执地说等Tywin死亡、真正出现问题时再处理，又称决定已经像石头般落定，不准Eddard再烦他。
blocks.literary_note_zh: “stone has been set”把政治任命说成已经固定的石头，Robert以不可逆性代替对风险的回应。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “Your Grace, may I speak frankly?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard请求允许自己坦率直言。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段围绕Jaime是否可信展开，从Kingsguard誓言追溯到Trident与Lannister靠假效忠进入King’s Landing的历史。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “I seem unable to stop you,” Robert grumbled. They rode through tall brown grasses.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert抱怨自己似乎从来阻止不了Eddard说真话。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段围绕Jaime是否可信展开，从Kingsguard誓言追溯到Trident与Lannister靠假效忠进入King’s Landing的历史。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “Can you trust Jaime Lannister?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard直接问Robert是否能够信任Jaime。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段围绕Jaime是否可信展开，从Kingsguard誓言追溯到Trident与Lannister靠假效忠进入King’s Landing的历史。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “He is my wife’s twin, a Sworn Brother of the Kingsguard, his life and fortune and honor all bound to mine.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert说Jaime是Cersei的双胞胎兄弟，又是Kingsguard的Sworn Brother，生命、财富和荣誉都与自己绑定。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert列举亲属、誓言和利益绑定作为可信证据；这些恰恰与Jaime过去服务Aerys时的结构相似。
blocks.background_zh: **誓言：** Kingsguard成员宣誓以生命保护国王；Jaime现服务Robert，但曾以同样身份服务Aerys。

blocks.original_text: “As they were bound to Aerys Targaryen’s,” Ned pointed out.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard提醒Robert，Jaime过去也以同样誓言与Aerys绑定。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard只用一行指出这种相似，整个信任论证立即面对反例。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “Why should I mistrust him? He has done everything I have ever asked of him. His sword helped win the throne I sit on.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert反问为何不信任Jaime，因为他一直服从命令，手中剑还帮助Robert取得王位。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段围绕Jaime是否可信展开，从Kingsguard誓言追溯到Trident与Lannister靠假效忠进入King’s Landing的历史。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: His sword helped taint the throne you sit on, Ned thought, but he did not permit the words to pass his lips. “He swore a vow to protect his king’s life with his own. Then he opened that king’s throat with a sword.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard心想那把剑也玷污了王位，却只说Jaime曾发誓以生命保护国王，最后却亲手杀了国王。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard内心的“玷污王位”没有说出，显示他即使获得坦率许可仍控制措辞，以免谈话彻底破裂。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “Seven hells, someone had to kill Aerys!” Robert said, reining his mount to a sudden halt beside an ancient barrow. “If Jaime hadn’t done it, it would have been left for you or me.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert在古老barrow旁勒马，认为Aerys总要有人杀；若不是Jaime，就会轮到自己或Eddard。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert关注Aerys是否该死，Eddard关注谁有权杀他；二人争论的是结果正义与誓言义务的不同层级。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “We were not Sworn Brothers of the Kingsguard,” Ned said. The time had come for Robert to hear the whole truth, he decided then and there. “Do you remember the Trident, Your Grace?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard指出自己和Robert都不是宣誓护王的Kingsguard，并决定讲出完整往事，问Robert是否记得Trident。
blocks.literary_note_zh: “我们不是Kingsguard”精确指出角色差异；随后Eddard决定讲完整往事，谈话从抽象原则进入证词。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “I won my crown there. How should I forget it?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert说自己在那里赢得王冠，不可能忘记。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段围绕Jaime是否可信展开，从Kingsguard誓言追溯到Trident与Lannister靠假效忠进入King’s Landing的历史。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “You took a wound from Rhaegar,” Ned reminded him. “So when the Targaryen host broke and ran, you gave the pursuit into my hands. The remnants of Rhaegar’s army fled back to King’s Landing. We followed. Aerys was in the Red Keep with several thousand loyalists. I expected to find the gates closed to us.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard回忆Robert被Rhaegar所伤后把追击交给自己；Targaryen残军退向King’s Landing，Aerys与数千忠诚者守在Red Keep，他原以为城门会关闭。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段围绕Jaime是否可信展开，从Kingsguard誓言追溯到Trident与Lannister靠假效忠进入King’s Landing的历史。
blocks.background_zh: **历史背景：** Robert在Trident击败Rhaegar并负伤，随后由Eddard追击败军至King’s Landing。

blocks.original_text: Robert gave an impatient shake of his head. “Instead you found that our men had already taken the city. What of it?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert不耐烦地说，Eddard到达时己方军队已经攻下城市，并问这有什么关系。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段围绕Jaime是否可信展开，从Kingsguard誓言追溯到Trident与Lannister靠假效忠进入King’s Landing的历史。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “Not our men,” Ned said patiently. “Lannister men. The lion of Lannister flew over the ramparts, not the crowned stag. And they had taken the city by treachery.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard纠正那不是Robert的军队，而是Lannister军；城墙上飘的是狮旗，不是王冠雄鹿，而且城市靠背叛夺取。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard反复纠正代词“our men”：胜利叙事中的阵营归属被他拆开，Lannister并非从战争开始就属于Robert。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: The war had raged for close to a year. Lords great and small had flocked to Robert’s banners; others had remained loyal to Targaryen. The mighty Lannisters of Casterly Rock, the Wardens of the West, had remained aloof from the struggle, ignoring calls to arms from both rebels and royalists. Aerys Targaryen must have thought that his gods had answered his prayers when Lord Tywin Lannister appeared before the gates of King’s Landing with an army twelve thousand strong, professing loyalty. So the mad king had ordered his last mad act. He had opened his city to the lions at the gate.
blocks.explanation_zh: 叙述回顾战争近一年时Lannisters始终中立，直到Tywin带一万二千人假称效忠抵达城门；Aerys把这当成神助，亲自把城市向狮子开放。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tywin在双方呼吁中保持中立，最后以假效忠进入城门；Aerys的“最后疯狂行为”是把背叛者误认成救援。
blocks.background_zh: **文本事实：** Lannisters在战争大部分时间保持中立，最后Tywin率一万二千人假称效忠，Aerys因此打开城门。

blocks.original_text: “Treachery was a coin the Targaryens knew well,” Robert said. The anger was building in him again. “Lannister paid them back in kind. It was no less than they deserved. I shall not trouble my sleep over it.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert说Targaryens同样熟悉背叛，Lannisters只是以同样货币偿还，他们罪有应得，不值得自己失眠。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert用“以同样货币偿还”把背叛变成对等报复，因而拒绝考虑攻城过程中的荣誉问题。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “You were not there,” Ned said, bitterness in his voice. Troubled sleep was no stranger to him. He had lived his lies for fourteen years, yet they still haunted him at night. “There was no honor in that conquest.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard苦涩地说Robert并不在场；自己十四年来仍被那次无荣誉的征服和长期隐瞒的事情在夜里纠缠。
blocks.literary_note_zh: “你不在场”把争论从宏观胜负拉回目击者经验；Eddard的失眠表明历史在他身上仍是未结束的当下。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “The Others take your honor!” Robert swore. “What did any Targaryen ever know of honor? Go down into your crypt and ask Lyanna about the dragon’s honor!”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert咒骂Eddard的honor，反问Targaryens何曾懂荣誉，并叫他去crypt询问Lyanna。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段让Robert与Eddard以报复和荣誉两套价值解释同一场征服；Eddard的目击证词最终被笑话化，使他再次意识到Hand的无力。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “You avenged Lyanna at the Trident,” Ned said, halting beside the king. Promise me, Ned, she had whispered.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard说Robert已经在Trident为Lyanna复仇，同时在心中听见她当年的低语“Promise me, Ned”。
blocks.literary_note_zh: “Promise me, Ned”以内心回声打断公开对话；文本事实是Lyanna曾提出承诺，但承诺内容截至此处仍未说明。
blocks.background_zh: **无剧透提示：** Eddard记起Lyanna说“Promise me, Ned”，但当前文本没有解释承诺内容。

blocks.original_text: “That did not bring her back.” Robert looked away, off into the grey distance. “The gods be damned. It was a hollow victory they gave me. A crown . . . it was the girl I prayed them for. Your sister, safe . . . and mine again, as she was meant to be. I ask you, Ned, what good is it to wear a crown? The gods mock the prayers of kings and cowherds alike.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert说复仇没有带回Lyanna，王冠是空洞胜利；他真正祈求的是她安全归来并重新属于自己，诸神却同样嘲弄国王与牧牛人。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert承认王冠是hollow victory：他赢得政治目标却失去真正祈求的人，胜利因此无法满足欲望。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “I cannot answer for the gods, Your Grace . . . only for what I found when I rode into the throne room that day,” Ned said. “Aerys was dead on the floor, drowned in his own blood. His dragon skulls stared down from the walls. Lannister’s men were everywhere. Jaime wore the white cloak of the Kingsguard over his golden armor. I can see him still. Even his sword was gilded. He was seated on the Iron Throne, high above his knights, wearing a helm fashioned in the shape of a lion’s head. How he glittered!”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard说自己无法替诸神回答，只能讲述进入throne room时所见：Aerys倒在血中，龙骨凝视，Jaime披白斗篷和金甲坐在Iron Throne上。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 金色Jaime、白斗篷、血泊Aerys和墙上龙骨形成强烈色彩与垂直空间；“How he glittered”既写华丽也带道德讽刺。
blocks.background_zh: **文本事实：** Eddard进入throne room时，Aerys已死，Jaime仍穿Kingsguard白斗篷并坐在Iron Throne上。

blocks.original_text: “This is well known,” the king complained.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert抱怨这件事人人都知道。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段让Robert与Eddard以报复和荣誉两套价值解释同一场征服；Eddard的目击证词最终被笑话化，使他再次意识到Hand的无力。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “I was still mounted. I rode the length of the hall in silence, between the long rows of dragon skulls. It felt as though they were watching me, somehow. I stopped in front of the throne, looking up at him. His golden sword was across his legs, its edge red with a king’s blood. My men were filling the room behind me. Lannister’s men drew back. I never said a word. I looked at him seated there on the throne, and I waited. At last Jaime laughed and got up. He took off his helm, and he said to me, ‘Have no fear, Stark. I was only keeping it warm for our friend Robert. It’s not a very comfortable seat, I’m afraid.’ ”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard继续回忆自己骑马穿过龙骨之间，在沉默对峙中看着Jaime膝上染有王血的金剑；最终Jaime笑着起身，说只是替Robert把不舒服的王座坐暖。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard几乎不说话，只靠骑马进入、军队涌入和等待迫使Jaime离座；沉默在这段记忆中比指控更有压力。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: The king threw back his head and roared. His laughter startled a flight of crows from the tall brown grass. They took to the air in a wild beating of wings. “You think I should mistrust Lannister because he sat on my throne for a few moments?” He shook with laughter again. “Jaime was all of seventeen, Ned. Scarce more than a boy.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert放声大笑，惊起草中乌鸦；他认为Jaime当时只有十七岁，只在王座坐了片刻，不足以成为不信任理由。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert的笑惊起乌鸦，环境对他的轻松产生不祥反响；他又用Jaime只有十七岁缩小Eddard所见的象征冒犯。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “Boy or man, he had no right to that throne.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard坚持无论男孩还是成人，Jaime都没有权利坐上王座。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段让Robert与Eddard以报复和荣誉两套价值解释同一场征服；Eddard的目击证词最终被笑话化，使他再次意识到Hand的无力。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: “Perhaps he was tired,” Robert suggested. “Killing kings is weary work. Gods know, there’s no place else to rest your ass in that damnable room. And he spoke truly, it is a monstrous uncomfortable chair. In more ways than one.” The king shook his head. “Well, now I know Jaime’s dark sin, and the matter can be forgotten. I am heartily sick of secrets and squabbles and matters of state, Ned. It’s all as tedious as counting coppers. Come, let’s ride, you used to know how. I want to feel the wind in my hair again.” He kicked his horse back into motion and galloped up over the barrow, raining earth down behind him.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert用“杀国王很累”继续开玩笑，又说Iron Throne确实处处不舒服；他宣布这项秘密可以忘掉，厌烦国事并催Eddard赛马追风。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert把Iron Throne的“不舒服”同时当作身体笑话和王权负担，却仍用赛马逃离具体制度问题。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: For a moment Ned did not follow. He had run out of words, and he was filled with a vast sense of helplessness. Not for the first time, he wondered what he was doing here and why he had come. He was no Jon Arryn, to curb the wildness of his king and teach him wisdom. Robert would do what he pleased, as he always had, and nothing Ned could say or do would change that. He belonged in Winterfell. He belonged with Catelyn in her grief, and with Bran.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard没有立即跟随，感到语言已经耗尽且深深无力；他怀疑自己为何来到这里，认为自己不是能约束Robert、教他智慧的Jon Arryn，真正属于Winterfell、Catelyn和Bran身边。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard的无力来自语言失效：他讲出珍藏十四年的完整证词，Robert却只听见一个可以笑过便忘的少年恶作剧。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。

blocks.original_text: A man could not always be where he belonged, though. Resigned, Eddard Stark put his boots into his horse and set off after the king.
blocks.explanation_zh: 但人不可能永远留在自己所属之地；Eddard认命地催马追赶国王。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 结尾区分belonging与duty：Eddard知道自己属于Winterfell，却仍必须追随国王，认命不是认同。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观设定；重点在Robert与Eddard的价值冲突、旧日战争记忆、王国军权安排或人物主观判断。
